wc_history_home: Colombia have appeared at seven World Cups, with their best performance coming in 2014 when they reached the quarter-finals, won their group, and James Rodriguez claimed the Golden Boot with six goals.
wc_history_away: Ghana qualified for four World Cups (2006, 2010, 2014, 2022), with their best run in 2010 when the Black Stars reached the quarterfinals in South Africa before losing to Uruguay on penalties, becoming only the third African nation to reach that stage.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Explosive wide attacking threat through Luis Diaz and pace on the counter-attack
Disciplined defensive mid-block that frustrates possession-heavy opponents
Creative set-piece and chance creation from James Rodriguez as a free-roaming number ten
strengths_away: Explosive pace and direct running from Williams and Semenyo in attack
Compact defensive structure that is hard to break down
Strong qualifying campaign with 8 wins from 10 matches building cohesion
team_style_home: Colombia under Nestor Lorenzo operate in a compact 4-2-3-1/4-3-3 mid-block system, defending deep and attacking through rapid vertical transitions and wide pace, particularly via Luis Diaz on the left flank.
team_style_away: Under Carlos Queiroz, Ghana set up in a compact 4-2-3-1 or 3-4-2-1 that shifts into a defensive mid-block out of possession, with Thomas Partey anchoring midfield and Semenyo and Inaki Williams exploiting space on the counter-attack.
recent_news: Ghana will be without Kojo Oppong Peprah due to an injury picked up in training. Colombia remains undefeated in the World Cup, having conceded just two goals in their last six games.
context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
